question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the regulatory framework for OTC foreign exchange derivative contracts involving INR in India?

answer.text: Authorised Dealers must ensure that OTC contracts involving INR (other than NDDCs) are for the purpose of hedging
OTC contracts involving INR may be entered into for speculative purposes by any person
Only exchange-traded currency derivatives require hedging purpose; OTC has no such restriction
RBI has no regulatory oversight over OTC foreign exchange derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While offering an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract involving INR (other than NDDCs) to a user, Authorised Dealers must ensure that the contract is for the purpose of hedging. This is mandated under FEMA regulations. **The Trap:** This hedging requirement applies specifically to OTC contracts involving INR. Exchange-traded derivatives are accessible without demonstrating underlying exposure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedging requirements for OTC vs ETD are frequently tested in Series I and are also relevant to Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This regulatory difference is a key advantage of exchange-traded currency derivatives — participants can take positions without proving underlying exposure, unlike OTC INR derivatives.
